txt: *Pedagogical* somehow. Brash. It must be yours for you to be able to read it. These are the lines of someone who has written by hand *a lot* and has developed a style only they themselves -- or *you yourself* -- can decipher.
... and it's not a shaker. It's nothing. He's holding nothing.
...and then accelerated straight into the fence.
...before heading south. Must have been in a hurry.
1) Standard work boot, steel reinforced toes, no 46. Just like Titus was wearing in his booth. This is the big dick -- Titus Hardie. The one with the ball cap on his head.
1) Standard work boot, steel reinforced toes, no 46.
2) Standard work boot, steel reinforced toes, no 44. Either the blond muscular guy, Glen, or the young guy with a plectrum around his neck.
2) Standard work boot, steel reinforced toes, no 44.
3) Hobnailed work boot, steel reinforced toes, no 43. The inked banger perhaps?
3) Hobnailed work boot, steel reinforced toes, no 43.
4) Standard work boot, no 45 or 46... Theo, the old smoker. You think you even see a tiny fleck of cigarette ash inside the print...
4) Standard work boot, no 45 or 46?
5) Another standard work boot, reinforced toes, no 44. Same as before -- either the musician Eugene, or the muscle-bound blond Glen.
5) Another standard work boot, steel reinforced toes, no 44.
6) An aberration -- light as air. Even pace. Same make of boot, but no 41.
6) Light as air. Same make of boot, but no 41. Small like a rat? Shanky!
7) The glowing outline of a standard work boot, no 46. But the imprints are *twice* as deep as the others -- the weight exceeds 200 kilograms.
7) The glowing outline of a standard work boot, no 46. The imprints are *twice* as deep as the others -- the weight exceeds 200 kilograms. Fat-Angus -- carrying something?

72%. With a weapon that fires jacketed ammunition -- likely a rifle. This is a good short distance, but not too short. The perpetrator aimed with their back against the railing, or possibly kneeling for precision. This would explain why it only took them one shot.

72%. With a weapon that's good for medium range, like a rifle or sports pistol. This is a good short distance, but not too short. The perpetrator aimed with their back against the railing, or possibly kneeling for precision. This would explain why it only took them one shot.

72%. With an antique weapon that fires military-grade ammunition -- a Belle-Magrave rifle, for example. This is a good short distance, but not too short. The perpetrator aimed with their back against the railing, or possibly kneeling for precision. This would explain why it only took them one shot.
8) And yet another standard work boot, no 44. There's an aberration in the pattern of the sole, however. The right sole is smoother, more worn.
8) Another standard work boot, no 44. There's an aberration in the pattern of the sole. The right sole is smoother, more worn. Curious... A missing *eighth* Hardie boy.
A bridge with loose nails and rot-infested wood that creaks in the wind. A construction code violation if there ever was one!
A building like this must have multiple doors serving various functions. Perhaps a basement access?
A cold sea wind blows away the figures.
A fine web of scarring covers the back of your right hand, but none of it is recent.
A fleet -- the combined armies of Occident and Graad, with Mesque volunteers. A five-nation army. Hundreds of vessels.
A glowing light on the third floor of the Whirling-in-Rags. With binoculars you would see a young woman's shape move behind the glass, her limbs long and slender.
A grand expanse of water reaches over the bay and to the horizon. Golden grey in the noon light...
A grand expanse of water reaches over the bay and to the horizon. Grey and pale violet in the morning light...
A grand expanse of water reaches over the bay and to the horizon. Wine dark in the evening light...
A great force from the northeast fired into the city. Heavy artillery shelled the coastline, fired from the water -- a straight shot into Revachol.

A host of men, probably in everyday clothes -- ragged from the conflict and covered in dust. They were not sitting (a common practice for executions in some nations), as demonstrated by the height level of the bullet holes.

A jigsaw of broken shards falls into place in front of you: a ghostly reconstruction of the stained glass window. Before it was shattered there was an older woman beneath the younger one -- and a text, a *leitmotiv* below them both.
A little something that your visual cortex has put together while you were away.
A little something that your visual cortex has put together while you were busy solving the case.
A long time has passed since the moment of this fusillading. Rain and brine have since washed all the blood away. Not a trace remains.

A luminous wheel of pleasure and all things bright, its wooden frame creaking in the wind. Twelve red cabins form a circle that stretches from the engines below to the flocks of seagulls up in the sky.
A powerful gust of wind coming in from the bay rattles the windowpane.
A ray of backward motion explodes from the man's mouth: through the window, to the roof outside, widening into a radius -- A prime.
A row of ghostly shades facing a crumbling wall, with another seven shades standing ten metres from them. A single person stands on the sideline.
A row of ghostly shades stand facing the wall. There are many of them, a dozen at least, their heads lowered and eyes blindfolded. It's quiet. No sound, no movement.
A shoe cupboard just off to the right? Have you been to this place?
A smoking assailant who favours Tioumoutiri to Astra or Drouin? Cigarette butts are part of the campfire set -- they're also everywhere.
A suit of armour like this would have been manufactured with a particular person's physique in mind. You should ask for whom this suit was fitted.
A vague sense of disappointment fills you. The glass shimmers as if taunting you with its secrets.
A young woman in her twenties. There is care, effort, and a *smile*, you think -- although that is not something you can read from someone's handwriting.

According to your map of the district, this shot could have come from a wide angle of locations -- starting with the northern edge of the abandoned boardwalk, ending with an islet in the bay. Let's call them B prime.
Affirmative.
Again with this. You're not some kind of super-detective. Question people, bust a lip open. Leave this glass cathedral of ballistics to the smart boys.
All you can see is the fact that her skin is a different colour from yours. That's literally all. You're gonna have to run with that.
An *abrasion collar*! THIS is what we're after.
An aerostatic passes overhead -- the whiskers of its floodlights on the ground below. Kitsuragi's glasses light up as he looks to the sky. Two glowing circles.
And maybe, just maybe, you succeeded. We'll never know.
And then along the left side: APRÈS LE MONDE - LE GRIS; APRÈS LE GRIS - LE MONDE DE NOUVEAU.
Another set of awfully old bullet holes.
Are you entirely sure you're black as *fuck*? You should really check out that mirror thing. For now, go with she's black. It's important to the case.
Are you sure? You haven't ruled out the shot coming from here yet...
As a defensive measure locking off that side of the bay.
As you fold your fingers into a fist, you realize you could knock anyone out with one punch... the white ceramic gloves wrap around your digits comfortably.
As your head is ensconced in the ceramic helm, you realize --
B' for boardwalk; B'' for Land's End and B''' for the islet, detective. There may be smaller points in between, but those are too fine to zoom in on.
Back to the village -- then back here -- then back to the coast *again*... That must be 3 kilometres in total. Okay...
Backyard door? There must be another entrance to the east.
Ballistics. In Klaasje's room. Do it *properly*.
Below both women, in luminous blackletter: APRÈS LA VIE - MORT; APRÈS LA MORT - LA VIE DE NOUVEAU.
Below the damage, the weeks of decomposition, all the swollen indignity of mortality -- he was 42 years old?
Better late than never, detective. The whole world is dark and the tracks burn in it with strange beauty.
Bullseye. A snooper. And a damn good one.
But how will you get to the island?
Can he even see through those glasses?
Certainly, but you may want to hold on to the armour you have for long enough to properly analyse it, figure out its vulnerabilities.
Clearly. The motor carriage in question was a Coupris 40 -- mere minutes before it took a final voyage into the ocean.
Cold. From the East.
Condensation -- from stepping inside.
Congratulations, you smashed the window with your own shoe. Now you only have one. If you're lucky you can still find the other on the balcony outside. The door to it should be outside your room.
Congratulations, you smashed the window with your own shoe. Now you only have one. If you're lucky, you can still find the other on the balcony outside. The door to it should be outside your room.
Congratulations, you smashed the window with your own shoe.
Consuming food is mechanical process for him. He doesn't enjoy it, just goes through the motions and moves on.
Correct again. Sub-zero temperatures would preserve the tracks in a good state. The commotion here *could* have taken place a week ago.
Could be. The shape of the branch supports the theory.
Could this have been the killer's hideout, and this narrow window -- the point of origin of the shot that killed the mercenary?
Disintegrating into mathematics.
Do a headcount first -- connect these men to the tracks you saw in the yard -- chances are they're going to match.
Do you want to know the truth?
Don't forget his face: the bloated and reddened cheeks, the bulbous nose... This would be Cuno after 30 years of alcohol and substance abuse.
Don't forget his face: the bloated and reddened cheeks, the bulbous nose... This would be Cuno after 30 years of substance abuse, if he drank too.
Don't let him go! This could be your witness -- the balcony has a great view of the whole thing.
Don't think like a cop, cops don't solve cases. They slide nippers on the goons and take 'em to the big house, but they don't *solve* anything.
Droplets of rain fall on the wooden planks, the surrounding sand dunes. The clouds block out any rays of light...
Eight pairs of boots have shuffled back and forth in the mud.
Exactly, the missing lady driver who was running the drug trade. Does it mean that the Hardie boys are involved in the drug trade as well?
Exactly. But none of this is news to you. You've stood there for about four seconds, not saying anything -- time to move on...
Exactly. You've stood there for about four seconds, not saying anything. Hit them with questions -- where's the eighth Hardie?
Fine. Don't impress them with ballistics.
For servicing an aerostatic battleship in the atmosphere? Or a fortification, like a seafort in the bay?
For seven days the cadaver is quite well preserved. Surprisingly so -- there's no deflation, no apparent fly larvae in the abdomen.
Forget about their games. You've mapped out the characters -- reading the footprints in the yard should be easier now.
From ballistics, it could easily have been a Triangong too.
From the roof outside -- location A prime. The glass fractures around the bullet hole. Shards face inwards like a corona behind the woman's back...
From wounds sustained over two, maybe more decades. Dispersal and accumulation indicates long and active combat duty.
Glad to have been of service. Now you know -- the locals are keeping tabs on you.
Gone -- like a skipping stone beneath the surface.
Good luck, these look darker than welding glasses.
Great news -- the boat is big enough for a grown man like you to fit underneath it in a supine position.
Guess so. Just be careful out there! One false step could cost you dearly!
Have you noticed how much taller he is than you?
He *knows* correctly. It is too small.
He could have easily disappeared into the sea through that hole. And you would have never found him.
He does seem frail, gaunt for his age, more like 75 than 65. Trouble putting on weight could mean cancer.
He knows it's hard to discern sex from a person's gait.
He looks worried.
He means beyond the coming days and weeks -- or the grasp of newspaper and radio reports that may trickle to your desk if ever you return to Precinct 41. In the far future.
He means: the pipe must be coming from *somewhere* in the building.
He might be right. Two hundred kilograms of living weight *is* unlikely.
He points toward the waterline with his bottle.
He wants you to open fire on the mercenaries before he does. He's waiting for you and your partner to be the shield.
He would have a good view of the tribunal from here. It's not just empty  boasting.
He's aiming for the eye-slot in Ruud's helmet. An extremely difficult shot...
He's assessing the situation. How long ago was it abandoned.
He's drunk. Drunk fighters over-correct. Move right -- he aims further right. Get him to overshoot!
He's not imagining it.
He's practically admitting to it. Just one thing -- the rifle does not have a scope.
He's right. That place is like a fortress. It's already almost impenetrable.
He's trying to find a straight line of sight before the rifleman can take you out. In the background, the leader is still on fire...
He's trying to find a straight line of sight before the rifleman can take you out. In the background, the leader staggers...
He's trying to find a straight line of sight before the rifleman can take you out.
Height: 170-175 cm, curly hair, stout build. Age: approximately 50-60 years.
Helium headlights would improve the range and quality of the visual field a lot.
Her eyes move up and down your person as though taking your measurements. Her attention is *scientific*.
Hey! Did someone say something already? No? Yes? Who cares! Upstairs, in Klaasje's bedroom -- the window the bullet came through. Analyse that!
His eyes are tracing an invisible line back and forth from your jacket to his companion.
Hmm hmm hmm... I'm sure we didn't get *everything*. There's always *something*...
Hmm... the print in some of these is pretty small though. This person has good eyesight.
Honestly, he does kind of look like you after all.
How convenient that they're stranded like beached whales at that roundabout.
How old are *you*? That's where this is going. Forty-five thousand litres of raw alcohol has left its disfigurements. What lies beneath, you wonder.
I don't know? Sleeping? What do people do under boats? This is merely a measurement from your visual cortex, do with it what you want.
I guess so.
I know! You should find the gun that shot this bullet.
I'm just a representation of your mental faculties... piecing together any available information. You should probably ask the lieutenant.
If there's any doubt, consult your trusty Dick Mullen book and reconnect with the real you, him. Dick Mullen. You. Him. Youse. Modus: Mullen.
Impossible to tell. Could also have been an adolescent. The gait is undeveloped.
In conclusion: these seven are the actors on the crime scene. The footprints were theirs, but there's a *discrepancy*.
In the darkness, you can barely make out the falling snow. It settles, making the night still quieter.
In the reeds, where he's pointing, you see a flash of white -- *porcelain* white.
In the reeds, where the phasmid was hiding, you see a little flash of white -- *porcelain* white.
In what must have been Semenine.
Indeed, there *was* a possible sniper's nest underneath the boardwalk but no one had been there in months, if not years.
Indeed, they all stood in a row here and looked at the tree.
Indeed. From this angle it does look like the neck isn't going to take much more. Being dead for a week has all but liquefied his muscles.
Indeed. There was no evidence of a sniper's nest in that location. The distance is too great and water vapour would have made the shot almost impossible.
Is it? They didn't even bother to change boots. Putting them on the scene is easy; maybe even *too* easy.
Is there something here that would indicate a sniper used this place as a nest for taking the shot? Just some urban detritus, a bottle and a dilapidated old comms tower.
Islet, technically. But yes. It looks more and more probable.
Isn't this something an industrial worker would wear?
It could be connected to the barred door upstairs.
It had to be, he's already proven capable of taking a far more complicated shot from here.
It is not impossible.
It is possible that it came from B prime (the boardwalk), B double prime (Land's End), or B triple prime (the islet).
It is too large for a bullet yet too small for a piece of furniture. You're looking for something heavy and larger than your fist.
It is. The fucking island awaits.
It leads to a side-building, adjacent to this one. The old building next to this, half ruined. Whatever is behind it must be older.
It may be a while before you have all the pieces. In the meantime, you should analyse the armour, figure out its vulnerabilities.
It may have been used to service an aerostatic battleship in the atmosphere? Or a fortification, like a seafort in the bay.
It seems like the brackets broke inside.
It suggests the bullet came from the extreme upper quadrant of possible angles -- from a point beyond the roof: B'''.
It was dirtier there. After the janitor's closet. She avoids the end of the hallway.
It wasn't *merde*. The shot was at least 23 metres. Probably 24 and then some. Nothing to be embarrassed about.
It wasn't *whorehouse of shit*. The shot was at least 23 metres. Probably 24 and then some. Nothing to be embarrassed about.
It's a good place to hide, but doesn't look like anyone has stayed here in a while.
It's a peninsula. She might be *trapped*.
It's about four-and-a-half kilometres from your position, you estimate.
It's about the right size, and the tyre marks look like they came from the skinny tyres frequently found on that motor carriage.
It's close enough to the latch up there -- one can slide it open and just take it. Surely not a coincidence.
It's dark, very dark... faint lights flicker at the edge of your vision. It's rare for the order to be carried out at night...
It's definitely a good place to hide. You barely found it yourself.
It's definitely not holding. Those matchboxes are stacked so haphazardly it's like they *want* them to collapse.
It's glowing in the dark night. The lights are on. Klaasje must be in there... looks like she's kept her promise not to go out at night anymore.
It's going to be awfully close to the already existing buildings. Almost wall to wall, practically integrating them into the youth centre.
It's late. There is no light on the third floor of the Whirling-in-Rags. No shape moves behind it. Klaasje must be out on the town.
It's light here -- but dark in the yard at night.
It's no older than a day, or two. No mould yet.
It's not a shaker. It's nothing. He's holding nothing. It is but an imitation.
It's not the roof -- and you would have found it had it been on the coast, in either B prime or B double prime, which leaves...
It's shaking the building's foundation. The floor twists...
It's waiting for you there.
Judging by the size of the impact and the parallel lines of burnt rubber, the cause was probably a motor vehicle.
Just for the record, you look nothing like the man on the document.
Just one thing remains unclear: the rifle does not seem to have a scope.
Just some tasteless advertising at the bottom of a canal. No use rubbernecking now.
Like the green shoe that's on the hat rack in the corner, which -- coincidentally -- is missing its friend.
Many are still there to this day. If you squint, you can just barely see the shadow on the water, far in the northeast. Cannons still ready to placate Revachol.
Many tall buildings, belonging to a district whose name escapes you.
Maybe if you extrapolate all possible points of origin first, the thread will make sense?
Maybe it's just a storm drain for the sewer?
Maybe more than 12? No. Eight pairs of boots have shuffled back and forth in the mud.
Maybe you keep failing because you *suck*?
Maybe. But hey, you've stood there for about four seconds, not saying anything. Now is a good time to hit them with questions -- where's the eighth Hardie?
Maybe. Probably.
Measuring things is important. How did she get the size?
More likely a projectile than a held object. There are no fragments on the floor from pulling a tool back in after impact.
More old bullet holes, at least half a century. A different era altogether.
More old bullet holes, half a century at least, from the Revolution.
Moreover, the boots were size 37. Tiny. There are too many discrepancies in all this.
My prediction? It will be at least two days.
No, it's not. No matter how you crane your neck, you can't see the whole of the Whirling-in-Rags, and certainly not the relevant segment of the top floor.
No, these tracks are not interesting at all. Let the street sweeper just sweep them away.
No, wait -- sorry. My bad. There's a rotting dog carcass under there and old fish too. You wouldn't fit next to the mummified canine's mausoleum.
No, you don't. You're a human measuring instrument, almost entirely intellectual.
No. That won't be necessary. Look closer. The gun, all three barrels, red and blue light shining through -- it's not loaded.
No. We're not failing this. You didn't get shot for nothing. Close your eyes and open them -- you can do this. You've done it before.
No. You feel there's something important here.  The embrasure. Look again...
None that you've found thus far. But that doesn't mean there aren't any.
Nothing in the history of things that *are* has ever *been* as monolithic as Jean-Luc Measurehead right now. He is an obelisk. A towering phallic obelisk of willpower.
Nothing scares you now.
Nothing, just the edge of the crack sparkles in the dark. There may have been a writing here but you cannot make out what it said.
Nothing.
Now? You'd have to follow the tracks to be sure.
Obviously.
Of course she won a beauty pageant. She's very symmetrical.
Of course, there were eight tracks! But there are only *seven* Hardie boys.
Of course. It's where the threads all meet...
Of course. The village on the coast. Let's go.
Of course. When you get the time, you could take another look at those tracks in the yard. See if they fit this band of miscreants.
Of course.
Oh god...
Oh, pardon me then. I forgot just how *cool* you were.
Okay. Seek not the truth. Cower in barbarism, you 58-year-old man-child.
On the photo in your hands -- the dead man's skin is studded with stars. Tens, hundreds of them, littering his dead skin.
One can't see into the Union box from the yard *outside* -- that hawthorn is too thick.

One kilometre away. A point beyond the docks, on an islet in the bay. The fort is ruined but the perpetrator may have found a stable spot on the beaches surrounding it, where the concrete crumbles into the sea -- as you saw in the coin operated viewer...
One kilometre away. An unlikely point of origin, beyond the docks somewhere -- on an islet in the Bay of Martinaise perhaps? There *are* islets there, badly charted as they may be.
One of the tall buildings overlooking the roundabout -- it would give them a read on the entire quarter.
One of the tall buildings overlooking the roundabout, most likely. That would give them a read on the entire quarter.
One-point-two kilometres away. The least likely of these positions, let's say 3%. A truly skilled sniper could have done it, possibly from a tent? No... too far fetched.
Only three storeys stand where nine to twelve once did. Restoration has failed. What the shelling took out was never re-built.
Oops...
Outside the window -- another day. The beach sand soaks up the drizzling rain, growing darker and darker.
Outside the window -- desolation. Snow falls softly onto the coastal sand, where it melts almost instantly. On rooftops, it lingers.
Outside the window -- the day is clear, and as mild as can be in early spring.
Point X would contradict the woman's testimony, rendering the entire proposition void. These figures would be wiped out, detective.
Pretty much. But, hey, at least you found your shoe.
Put the numbers out there. This *will* impress them.
Recent. Self-inflicted.
Remember the tyre tracks in Martinaise? This is where they were leading.
Remember, the vitreous enamel material acts as a kinetic re-distributor. It's meant to stop bullets. Wear it, observe its properties. See if there's a *weakness* in the design.
Remember, this is a highly specialized kinetic re-distributor meant to stop bullets. Wear it, observe its properties. See if there's a *weakness* in the design.
Ruby ruled this out vehemently. The shot would have been heard from downstairs -- where no one heard it. The likelihood of A' has fallen drastically. You may be looking for a sniper.
Ruination has come. The broken arches betray the once grand history of this building. It towered over the harbour, until *it* happened.

Seven hundred metres away. The likeliest of these B positions, 20% chance. A skilled sniper could have made the shot, provided he had a safe sniper's nest. Even with the light on inside, we're talking military training. At that distance the perpetrator would have had to take wind direction into account.
Seven men in combat uniforms and dark coats, holding automatic rifles aimed at the people. Soldiers from some side... but from which one?
She had a long time to think about it after. Standing on that roof -- staring at the glass...
She has changed the subject and won't let you get back to this.
She starts coughing. It's definitely a smoker's cough.
She was there that night -- she would have known precisely where the bullet hole was in the glass.
She's got a direct view to the backyard. You should interrogate her about the lynching.
Shoe size is 41-42, maybe 43. It could be a large-footed woman or a small-to-average-footed man. This is, unfortunately, the worst, most vague shoe size there is.
Side slip marks indicate that the vehicle was travelling up the crater at 35 km/h.
Sleeping. Was it not decided that you're a *hobocop* -- more hobo than cop -- or was that fine irony that your visual cortex somehow misunderstood?

Small figures of wise men, common men, worshippers walk up the stairs to stand at her feet. Secret servicemen -- Therriers -- stand in a row guarding her. It must have taken years to produce this work in all its dizzying detail.
Smooth reflective surface... are these the gloves you're looking for?
Snowflakes fall on the wooden planks and the surrounding sand dunes, covering the land with the white of innocence...
So?
Some of that rubber stuck to the tiles right in front of the Whirling-in-Rags. This is point A. The driver started there...
Some sort of countdown process is happening. That's all you have time to think...
Some waves come crashing against the boardwalk, shattering the mirage.
Someone's shot him in the heart. Interesting.
Something about the tracks? Suddenly all those tracks are so confusing... go with something else first!
Something you've done before.
Sound familiar? That matches the prints you observed at the scene.
Still speeding, the vehicle made a loop and vanished into the fog along the coast.
Still you see the glass sparkling out of the corner of your eye...
Stop looking at her, look around! What do you see?
Strange, he seems different in here. Less 'monolithic' somehow.

Ten metres away -- other shades are lined up in an orderly manner, automatic rifles primed. A gust of wind blows by -- the coats of the firing squad flap slowly in the breeze. A single person stands on the side.
That *rebel* who jumped the canal and broke the waterlock! This is their doing.
That does sound quite unlikely, yes. The big guy leading the scabs at the gates is *colossal*.
That merc was probably shot from a distance with a military-grade rifle. Otherwise everyone at the Whirling would have heard the shot downstairs.
That would be incredibly unsound at this moment.
That's a 28%, yes. In this model the shot *could* have come from a greater distance. Nothing excludes the possibility. Should we extrapolate to include *every* possible point of origin in Martinaise?
That's because you're a dick in cop's clothing. Underneath -- a snooper through and through.
That's correct. There is no visibility of any of the others.
That's enough to take another look at those tracks in the yard. See if they fit this band of miscreants.
That's half a *gram*, sir.
That's it. You got it.
That's right. And the canal, the bookstore, the harbour gates... This is a great vantage point -- for keeping an eye on *you*.
The Kojkos of Graad (Yugo, Zsiemsk, Shest et al) are what you would call, white, officer -- in a suspect description.

The abundance of bullet holes leads to two options: either an inordinate amount of executions were performed here, or they did not use a *conscience round* -- where only one soldier has the loaded rifle. Looks like this was a mass execution with everyone fully armed.
The bear looks oddly realistic. Is it taxidermy?
The black marks on the roof indicate that the vehicle *vaulted* from the crater to the roof of the shack.
The bronze ribbon twists around and within the branches in an intricately natural pattern... but there's something...
The bruises. Can you make that out in a scope?
The bruises. You can't make that out in a scope...
The bullet hole in Filippe's heart... he'd been shot in the heart. By him?
The coat, the hat, the swindle so crooked a yeggman couldn't break it.
The cold sea breeze stings your face as you step on the boardwalk. The body is gone, but something still lingers in the air. And high above it, against the gray...
The cold sea breeze stings your face as you step on the boardwalk. The body is gone, but something still lingers in the air. And high above it, against the stars...
The commandant -- the one who gives the order. Machine gun fire crackling through the air, the lights of the muzzle flashes dancing on his face...
The dicks, gum-heels, hawkshaws, peepers, sleuths -- men like Mullen and you.

The drawings are precise and look true to their pseudoscientific ambitions. One thing however -- it is not *entirely* free of throwbacks in the phylogenetic tree. His large jaw, for example, could be a trait indicative of criminalism. Also his ear lobes could be smaller.
The driver proceeded to back out of the yard, barely stopping before hitting the adjacent building...
The edge of a floorboard next to it looks scratched.

The escutcheon on her throne says: Irene the Navigator. She is depicted as an older woman wearing thick rimmed eye-glasses, holding a golden *reichsapfel* in one hand and a scepter in the other. This is the queen Her Innocence Dei advised -- above, she herself is whole.
The estimate is correct. The incident probably occurred on Sunday evening.

The faint impressions of many footprints are also present, though it's impossible to tell what kind or how many. Suffice to say, the jacket spent some time on the ground before someone draped it over the railing...
The golden light melts away into the blue, glassy darkness of your mind. In it are two neon lit shapes: a man and a woman on the single bed.
The grand expanse of water is barely visible in the darkness of the night. The horizon is there, somewhere...
The hardened vitreous enamel, at once sleek and light, adds a glow to your cheeks and a spring to your step. Just imagine what a full suit of this stuff could do for you!
The hardened vitreous enamel, at once sleek and voluptuous, adds a glow to your cheeks and a spring to your step. Just imagine what a full suit of this stuff could do for you!
The hook -- where is it? I can't see it.
The island in the bay.
The kinetic energy of the 9mm front-loaded bullet is distributed evenly across the chest piece and into his hands.
The kitchen -- the only part of the building you haven't thoroughly searched yet. It's worth a try!
The kitchen -- the only part of the building you haven't thoroughly searched yet.
The lack of alcohol swelling is a bonus. Though there's a *pinch* of that in your cheeks already.
The lieutenant is glad he got the floor plan right there.
The lieutenant's estimate is correct. The incident probably occurred on Sunday eve.
The light is off on the third floor of the Whirling-in-Rags. The young woman is gone who knows where. Safe, closed off. Her form no longer moves behind the glass there...
The light is off on the third floor of the Whirling-in-Rags. The young woman is gone, in Precinct 57. Safe, closed off. Her form no longer moves behind the glass there...
The light refracts. She takes out her lighter.
The light vanishes inside the concrete slit. The structure goes deep under the earth.
The lights of Martinaise shine coolly in the clear night.

The lights were on in here. Outside it was dark. It was like shooting fish in an aquarium -- a well lit aquarium. The victim opened his mouth to let the bullet in. Neither of them would have seen anything outside, in the darkness -- too busy with their own bodies.
The little wooden gondolas dangle in the wind, their seats empty and floors littered with old ice cream papers and discarded tickets...
The makings of a sniper's nest.
The man does not know the bullet has entered his brain. He never will. Death comes faster than the realization.
The man is kneeling, the woman is on her back. It's the night of March 4th and a shot has just been fired.
The man looks directly at the woman. The shot's possible directions converge in his mouth -- a ray cast from somewhere outside, entering his brain.
The morning sun rises beyond the horizon, radiating the first light of the day. The order was carried out at dawn.
The object that is in there stopped just short of the skull, in the encephalus, knocking this tiny fracture into the cranium...
The pale. The Mundi isola. The Occident. And then -- *Oranje*. The Old Old World.
The panel served as a take-off ramp.
The pleasure wheel stands still on the boardwalk, its twelve red cabins suspended in the air.

The ray cast from the man's mouth unravels into a fan of possible directions, all on the roof at first. The shot could have come from any of them. This is composite location A prime -- most likely of the origin points.
The red thread bisecting the room shows the trajectory of the bullet -- Klaasje has done some amateur ballistics here.
The red thread bisecting the room shows the trajectory of the bullet.
The salty water has corroded the ladder to nothing but two thin orange rails. In addition, there is no stable nest up there. It would have been useless.
The same two neon lit shapes -- a man and a woman. A ray of backward motion explodes from the man's mouth, through the window, to the roof outside, widening into a radius -- A prime.
The shards face outward. Whatever broke this window came from the inside.

The shards glimmer in the dark -- you see little pearls of light on the edges of the crack that splits the female figure. Something was written there... remains of broken letters line the emulsion. What it said, you do not know.
The shoe you found out on the balcony fits the hole almost as well as your foot. It would have also been heavy enough, if thrown with force.
The shoe you found out on the balcony fits the hole almost as well as your foot. It's similar to the green shoe that's on the hat rack in the corner, which -- coincidentally -- is missing its friend.
The shot had to come from a greater distance -- beyond A'. Should we extrapolate to include *every* possible point of origin in Martinaise?

The shot would have been a small miracle: 5% likelihood. There *is* an extremely narrow field of view from the bay to the window, between Rue de Saint Ghislaine 10 and 33A. The angle would have been extreme and access to the islets is questionable...
The sign broke the vehicle's fall into the canal.
The single green shoe you found fits the hole almost as well as your foot. It would have also been heavy enough, if thrown with force.
The slow movement of the branch in the wind and your shoulders directing the gun sync up, dancing hypnotically.
The sun blazes high up in the sky... baking the planks, the sand, your skin. The order was carried out in the afternoon.
The sun falls slowly toward the horizon... the last light of the day. The order was carried out in the evening.
The tenement acted as a defensive wall against the worst of the shelling. Until it was destroyed, and they had a direct firing line.
The tracks are as they have ever been. A bit more worn, perhaps. Fortunately, the street sweeper still hasn't noticed their presence.
The tyre tracks were left here by an unknown event that took place some days ago. It's a message, written in the language of burnt rubber.
The vehicle soared through the air, hitting the billboard and upsetting the posts. Then it continued its flight, taking the billboard with it.
The waves of the Martinaise inlet roll over the fallen remains of the building. The dark waters obscure the better part of the remains.
The white ceramic cuirass hugs your torso, shielding your vitals without adding much bulk to your form...
The whole shebang.
The wind is cold. From the east.
Then find the gun that shot it, and the person who that gun belongs to -- he used it to kill your victim. Neato!

Then you should prepare -- analyse this armour, figure out its vulnerabilities.
There are eight pairs of tracks in the mud. Three stick out, but...
There are eight pairs of tracks in the mud. Three stick out.
There are no collars on the barbell. This is a safety hazard.
There are two good candidates: the Coupris 40 and the Linnea G22.
There could've been. Then the rain and slush and wind washed it away. This was more than a week ago.
There is some kind of countdown happening, as it slowly processes the overwhelming brightness of the signal. The invertebrate is regaining control...
There was a mail delivery box in the plaza, near the corner of the bookstore.
There were boats there. You should ask around.
There were skiffs in the fishing village. You should ask around.
There's a ticket office made out of an old gondola, its wooden frame the same shade of red as the ones above. A sign mounted on its side declares: "TWENTY CENTIMS FOR THE RIDE OF A LIFETIME!"
There's a twisted logic in this mess... you see the ends of the ribbon, the way it flows, how it twists and turns... it's all starting to make sense. It'll be easier to remove now.
There's no echo -- and no answer.
There's no way it should stay up like that. How is the weight being distributed?
There's only three metres between you and the machine. If you keep her distracted for long enough, maybe...
There's reál, fifty in his palm.
There's rust and corrosion on the bars, they're foaming with it. And a small layer of white salt from the sea.
There's something about this guy that *matches* with a face in your head. A similar, but different face.
There's something on the sea ice there.
There, 1.2 kilometres over the cold water of the bay, blue from the distance and the air, you see the smallest rectangle, barely visible.
There, 1.2 kilometres over the cold water of the bay, through a thick snowstorm melting flake by flake in the waves you see the smallest rectangle, barely visible.
There, 1.2 kilometres over the wave-crossed bay, through heavy, dark grey curtains of rain you see the smallest rectangle, barely visible.
There, behind them, across the Delta -- the INSURCOM building. Coalition Government Insulindian Mission Control.
These are not the unusual, horizontal patterned soles you saw in the dust on the floor of the hidden room. They do, however, seem to be about the same size...
These are the hands on someone who's smoked for at least twenty years, as evidenced by the discolouration of fingers. Marks on your skin may point to past quarrels.
These look like the same tyre tracks I saw earlier -- in front of the Whirling-in-Rags.
These match the tyre tracks you saw earlier -- *your* tyre tracks.
They look pretty ancient...
They massed airships further down, in the bay of Revachol. The artillery was so powerful, the ships not only required gyroscopic stabilization -- they were anchored into the ocean floor as well.
They must have someone in an overlook position, near the gates...
They rise like electric obelisks in the night.
They stand, facing the wall... It's impossible to discern any details about their personality or background.
This *can* be navigated. The balls leave an almost imperceptible 'disruptions' in the fog. Use them to calculate where they hit next.
This could be a way into the apartment building the smoking man vanished into.
This is B triple prime -- on the island... A nest.

This is a trick. You've done it before. Pink is where the blood settled in the first hours postmortem. You can use it to see if the corpse has been tampered with -- does his position at the time of death match the discolouration?
This is concurrent with your doubts -- at the autopsy -- about the colouring being...
This is how I always talk. Move on. The swindle -- cracked it like a discount safe.
This is the Man with Sunglasses from the Whirling-in-Rags! But where are his sunglasses?
This is where the first shot will come from.
This one *could* work for seeing where the shot came from.

This panel usually closes the water lock, turning it into a bridge that lets you cross the canal -- but there's a crashed "Samaran butter" sign in the way. Pulling the lever *probably* won't do anything.
This tactful approach will rob you the glory, but hey, all I care about is angles and timetables. The loss is yours.
This was a good call. That guy probably thinks forensics is autism too.
Those arches acted as support for something greater than what you see now.
Time to really close the deal. Show them the bullet.

To warm his hands before he pulled the trigger? There are no markings on the ground for a tripod, he would have had to lie on a mattress. This is simply not a good spot for a 1.2 kilometre shot.
Too late to stop you. You've already got them all -- including the old bearded man who's smart enough to smoke instead of talking.

You could *follow* the tracks south. There seems to be a canal there. See where they went. (If you find the time.)
You could've been one of the good ones.
You count 216 perforations. Considering that nice, large number, a wave of pride washes over you, though you can't say why, precisely.
You don't know when to quit, do you?
You don't know. It's a miracle you can tell the prints apart as it is. The cold must have preserved them.
You have to go to the island. It's where the threads all meet...
You hear the rain's susurration across the sea water. The night shudders.
You know, I don't usually do this -- I just measure things, but...
You look down at the white ceramic sabatons hugging your arches and calves, surprised at how well they fit.
You may want to slow down. Don't tear into it like that.

You see the same two neon lit shapes -- a man and a woman. Only now a red thread bisects the room, pointing from the antenna outside, to the cupboard on the wall.
You see two, maybe three bushes, grown into a single thicket -- over a period of circa 50 years. It takes time for a hedge like that to form.
You should have a look at that window -- reconstruct the scene. It's right here, so near...
You should have another look at that window after this -- reconstruct the scene. It's right there.
You should return to the boardwalk now that the body has been taken away. There's something you need to see.
You should return to the boardwalk once the body has been taken away. There's something you need to see.
You would need a map for that -- a map of Martinaise.
You would still like the hypostasis marks in the neck to be a *bit* more pronounced.
You're a rough rider, but not in your early thirties. Do you want to know *the truth*?
You're not bad. It's as if the whole world darkens -- and the tracks burn in it with strange beauty.
You're not bad. It's as if the whole world darkens, everything else has a thin film of unimportance on it -- and the tracks burn in the middle of it, in a strange, beautiful way.
Your visual confirms: you could fit *two* more bodies in the ice bear fridge.
Your voice alone rings in the room. This visual forensics ability doesn't dispense shopping tips.
Your voice sounds hollow in the deserted room... you are alone.
